_help: OPTIONAL — only needed for the instruction-auto-apply eval (trigger_eval.py). Skip it entirely if your instruction files are always-on (applyTo: "**"), since auto-apply is then trivial. Replace every <...> placeholder. Keys prefixed with _ are ignored.
instruction_name: <name of the instruction file under test>
_signal_help: The canary token your instruction file emits when active. Add a line to that instruction file like: 'When these instructions are in effect, begin your reply with [[MY-INSTR-ACTIVE]].' If you omit signal, the fallback checks whether the instruction filename appears in the agent's output.
_prompts_help: Positive cases (should_trigger:true) should apply the instruction; negatives should not. context_file names a file in the prompt so an applyTo glob actually fires.

prompts.text: <a task that SHOULD apply this instruction>
<an off-target task that should NOT apply it>
